data.title: Mubadala Investment Company Emerges As Major Bitcoin ETF Holder
data.description: According to Odaily, Bloomberg Senior Analyst James Seyffart shared data on the X platform indicating that the Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund, Mubadala Investment Company, is currently the seventh-largest holder of BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF (IBIT), with holdings valued at $461.23 million. This development highlights the growing interest of traditional financial institutions in the cryptocurrency market, as they increasingly engage through investments in Bitcoin ETFs and similar avenues.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Overnight Reverse Repo Usage Hits Four-Year Low
data.description: According to Odaily, the usage of the Federal Reserve's overnight reverse repurchase (RRP) agreements continued to decline on Friday, reaching its lowest level in nearly four years. Approximately 22 counterparties deposited $58.8 billion through RRP, marking the lowest amount since April 2021. This is a decrease from the previous trading day's $67.8 billion. The number of counterparties also fell to its lowest since May 2020.

data.title: Google Allows Cryptocurrency Ads In UAE With Certification
data.description: According to Odaily, Google has announced that starting February 26, cryptocurrency exchanges and software wallet advertisers targeting the United Arab Emirates can run ads if they meet specific requirements and obtain certification.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Stance on Interest Rates Amid Strong Labor Market
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve official Logan stated that a robust labor market does not necessarily imply that the Federal Reserve can lower interest rates, even if inflation data improves. Logan emphasized the need for caution in adjusting interest rates, highlighting the significance of upcoming inflation data in the next few months. Additionally, attention must be paid to geopolitical and policy changes, as these factors are being carefully monitored for their impact on the economy.

data.title: Zelensky Open to Direct Talks with Russia If Consensus Reached
data.description: According to Odaily, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced on the 14th at the Munich Security Conference that he is prepared to engage in direct talks with Russia once Ukraine, the United States, and Europe reach an agreement on how to resolve the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine. Zelensky stated that Ukraine is ready to engage in any dialogue with the U.S. and its allies. He emphasized that if the U.S. and Europe provide specific responses to Ukraine's requests and a consensus is achieved, "we will be ready to engage in dialogue with Russia."

data.title: Robinhood Launches Solana-Based PYTH Token
data.description: According to the official announcement, Robinhood launched the Solana-based PYTH token.

data.title: Musk's xAI considers raising $10 billion at a $75 billion valuation
data.description: Musk's xAI considers raising $10 billion at a valuation of $75 billion.

data.title: Altcoin News: Altcoin Season Delayed as Retail Traders Remain Sidelined, Analysts Say
data.description: Crypto analysts are predicting a delayed altcoin season as retail traders show limited interest in altcoins, with memecoins dominating the current market frenzy. Despite some altcoins experiencing price rallies, the lack of growth in daily active wallets suggests the market is still in an early “speculative” phase, far from the widespread adoption seen during the 2021 bull run.Key Indicators Point to a Speculative Phase1. Declining Daily Active WalletsData from IntoTheBlock reveals a significant drop in daily active wallets for several altcoins compared to their 2021 peaks:Algorand (ALGO):Daily active wallets fell to 182,170 on February 10, with ALGO trading at $0.28.At its all-time high of $1.46 on December 20, 2021, Algorand had over 1.31 million active addresses.Chainlink (LINK):Daily active addresses dropped to 3,860 on February 10.During its peak of $46.71 on May 3, 2021, Chainlink had 11,280 active addresses.This decline in user activity indicates that altcoin price rallies are not yet supported by broader retail participation.2. Memecoin Frenzy Diverts AttentionMemecoins, known for their high-risk, high-reward nature, have captured a significant share of retail investor interest. For example:On February 14, a crypto trader made $28 million in profit by trading the “Broccoli” memecoin, inspired by Binance co-founder Changpeng Zhao’s dog.Speculation suggests the trader may have been an insider, highlighting the speculative and often opaque nature of memecoin trading.According to Nicolai Sondergaard, a research analyst at Nansen, the current cycle is marked by “higher levels of dispersion” among altcoins, with specific sectors and tokens outperforming others.What’s Holding Back Altcoin Season?1. Lack of Retail ParticipationThe absence of retail traders in the altcoin market is a key factor delaying the altcoin season. Marcin Kazmierczak, co-founder and COO of Redstone, notes:“Lower daily active addresses on most altcoins compared to 2021 peaks suggest we’re earlier in the cycle. Price recovery without matching daily active address growth indicates we’re likely in the initial speculative phase before widespread adoption kicks in.”2. Market Capitalization Remains LowThe total market capitalization of altcoins (excluding the top 10 cryptocurrencies) is currently near a three-month low of 277 billion, according to TradingView data. This represents a 77% decline from its peak market cap of 492 billion, which was recorded on November 10, 2021.When Will Altcoin Season Arrive?Analysts believe the altcoin season is still on the horizon but may look different from previous cycles. Key factors to watch include:Increased retail participation: A surge in daily active wallets could signal the start of a broader altcoin rally.Sector-specific growth: Certain sectors, such as decentralized finance (DeFi) or real-world assets (RWAs), may outperform others.Memecoin fatigue: As the memecoin frenzy subsides, retail traders may shift their focus back to altcoins with stronger fundamentals.Why This MattersThe delayed altcoin season highlights the evolving dynamics of the crypto market, where memecoins and speculative trading dominate retail interest. For long-term investors, this phase presents an opportunity to identify undervalued altcoins with strong fundamentals before the next wave of adoption, according to Cointelegraph.

data.title: Crypto News Today: U.S. States Race to Introduce Crypto Legislation: Bitcoin Reserves, Task Forces, and More
data.description: In a significant push to regulate and embrace the cryptocurrency industry, nine U.S. states have introduced crypto-related bills in February alone. These proposals range from creating Bitcoin reserves to establishing task forces aimed at shaping state policies on digital assets. This surge in legislative activity reflects the growing influence of the crypto industry, which spent nearly $250 billion supporting pro-crypto candidates during the 2024 federal and state elections.Here’s a breakdown of the latest developments across key states:North Carolina: Investing in Crypto Through ETPsOn February 10, North Carolina lawmakers introduced the NC Digital Assets Investments Act, spearheaded by House Speaker Destin Hall. The bill allows the state treasurer to invest public funds in cryptocurrency through exchange-traded products (ETPs) like ETFs, rather than directly holding digital assets.Key Provisions:ETPs must have a market capitalization of at least $750 billion over the past 12 months.Crypto investments cannot exceed 10% of the state’s total investment funds.Hall emphasized that this move positions North Carolina as a leader in technological innovation and aligns with former President Donald Trump’s vision of making the U.S. a global crypto hub. The bill has passed its first reading and is now under review by the Committee on Commerce and Economic Development.Michigan: Creating a Crypto ReserveMichigan joined the crypto legislative wave on February 13 with a bill introduced by Representatives Bryan Posthumus and Ron Robinson. The proposal aims to amend the state budget to allow the creation of a crypto reserve.Key Provisions:The state treasurer can allocate up to 10% of available funds to crypto investments.Cryptocurrencies can be held directly through secure custody solutions or via ETPs.The state can loan out crypto assets to generate additional returns, provided it doesn’t increase financial risk.Posthumus also voiced support for MichCoin, a hypothetical stablecoin backed by gold and silver, further highlighting Michigan’s ambition to compete with crypto-forward states like Texas. Both bills are currently in committee.New York: Establishing a Crypto Task ForceNew York lawmakers are focusing on understanding the broader implications of cryptocurrency adoption. A bill introduced in the Empire State proposes the creation of a 17-member crypto task force to examine the state of digital assets and their impact.Key Focus Areas:Crypto energy consumption and environmental impact.Comparison of New York’s crypto laws with those of other states.The restrictive BitLicense regime, which has driven many crypto businesses out of the state.New York City Mayor Eric Adams has been a vocal critic of the BitLicense, calling it a barrier to competitiveness. The task force, if enacted, will present its findings to the governor and state legislature by December 15, 2027. The bill is currently under review in the Senate committee.Texas: Expanding Its Bitcoin ReserveTexas, already a leader in the U.S. blockchain industry, is tweaking its earlier Bitcoin reserve bill to include other cryptocurrencies. The new bill, filed on February 12, removes the previous $500 million appropriation cap, allowing the state to invest unlimited funds in Bitcoin and other qualifying digital assets.Key Provisions:Cryptocurrencies must have a market capitalization of at least $500 billion over the past 12 months (currently, only Bitcoin meets this criterion).The bill eliminates restrictions on the amount Texas can allocate to its crypto reserve.State Senator Charles Schwertner expressed optimism about the bill’s passage, noting it is among the Senate’s top 40 priority bills.Other States Joining the Crypto WaveNorth Carolina, Michigan, New York, and Texas are not alone in their crypto legislative efforts. In February, several other states introduced bills to create Bitcoin reserves or allocate state funds to crypto-related investments:FloridaUtahOhioMissouriKentuckyHowever, not all proposals have been successful. Bitcoin reserve bills were previously rejected in North Dakota and Wyoming, despite the latter being considered a crypto-friendly state.The Road Ahead for Crypto LegislationWhile the momentum for crypto legislation is growing, the path to enactment is long and uncertain. Bills must navigate committees, revisions, and political negotiations before becoming law. Additionally, federal regulations on stablecoins and digital assets could provide a framework for states to align their policies.Why This Matters:The flurry of state-level crypto legislation highlights the increasing recognition of digital assets as a critical component of the financial landscape. These efforts aim to foster innovation, attract crypto businesses, and position states as leaders in the emerging digital economy, according to Cointelegraph.

data.title: Crypto News: SEC Crypto Task Force Engages Industry Leaders on Staking, ETFs, and Regulatory Clarity
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission’s (SEC) Crypto Task Force recently held a series of meetings with key players from the cryptocurrency and traditional finance sectors to address pressing regulatory issues, including staking, exchange-traded products (ETPs), and the classification of digital assets. The discussions, which took place in early February, involved prominent firms such as the Blockchain Association, Jito Labs, Multicoin Capital, Nasdaq, Andreessen Horowitz, and Sullivan & Cromwell.Key Discussion Points from the SEC Crypto Task Force Meetings1. Blockchain Association Advocates for Pro-Innovation RegulationOn February 4, the Blockchain Association, a leading crypto industry lobby group, presented six priority areas for the SEC to focus on:Establishing clear regulatory principles for digital assets.Adopting a pro-innovation approach for broker-dealers, custodians, and exchanges.Creating uniform standards for crypto ETPs.Ensuring staking protocols are not classified as securities.Reviewing and correcting past legal interpretations that may hinder industry growth.The association emphasized the need for a “retroactive” review process, echoing SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce’s recent statements.2. Staking in ETPs: A Potential Game-ChangerOn February 5, Jito Labs and Multicoin Capital met with the task force to discuss the inclusion of staking in ETPs. The firms argued that staking represents the “true nature” of proof-of-stake tokens like Ethereum (ETH) and Solana (SOL).This discussion comes after the SEC’s 2023 approval of spot Ether ETFs, which excluded staking rewards. Jito and Multicoin Capital expressed optimism that the SEC may reconsider staking for ETH and other crypto ETPs, particularly in light of new applications for a Solana-based ETP.3. Andreessen Horowitz Pushes for Clear Token ClassificationAlso on February 5, Andreessen Horowitz’s capital management group, AH Capital Management, met with the task force to discuss token classification, issuance, and the role of market intermediaries. The firm highlighted the need for regulatory clarity to foster innovation while ensuring compliance.4. Nasdaq Calls for Unified Trading VenuesOn February 6, Nasdaq representatives urged the SEC to clarify which venues are permitted to trade cryptocurrencies. They argued that non-securities digital assets should be allowed to trade alongside securities in the same venues to ensure consistent regulatory frameworks.5. Sullivan & Cromwell Discusses Blockchain and Securities LawOn February 7, bankruptcy law firm Sullivan & Cromwell, which previously served as counsel for FTX, met with the task force to discuss blockchain technology and its intersection with securities law. The firm’s representative, Colin D. Lloyd, emphasized the importance of addressing legal ambiguities in the crypto space.Why This Matters for the Crypto IndustryThe SEC’s engagement with industry leaders signals a potential shift toward more nuanced and innovation-friendly regulations. Key takeaways include:Staking in ETPs: A potential reversal of the SEC’s stance on staking rewards could open new opportunities for Ethereum, Solana, and other proof-of-stake tokens.Regulatory Clarity: Clear guidelines on token classification, trading venues, and market intermediaries could reduce uncertainty and encourage institutional participation.Pro-Innovation Approach: A focus on fostering innovation while ensuring compliance could help the U.S. maintain its position as a global leader in the crypto industry.As the SEC continues to refine its approach to digital asset regulation, the crypto industry will be closely watching for updates on staking, ETPs, and broader regulatory frameworks. These developments could have far-reaching implications for investors, exchanges, and blockchain projects alike, according to Cointelegraph.
